rent.note: Monthly snapshot, not live counts — every listings site's terms prohibit automated access. ZORI updates monthly; HUD FMR annually.

stats.label: Typical asking rent, Burlington metro (+1.6% in a year)

sources.name: Zillow Rentals
sources.good_for: The biggest single pool of professionally listed Burlington rentals, with the best filters and map search.
sources.gotcha: Pushes Zillow's own $35 application flow that some landlords never look at; duplicate listings inflate the counts.

sources.name: Apartments.com
sources.good_for: Best for the larger managed buildings and complexes — verified listings, 3D tours, and a for-rent-by-owner section.
sources.gotcha: The headline count sweeps in surrounding towns and counts units, not buildings; small local landlords barely show up here.

sources.name: Craigslist
sources.good_for: Where small local landlords still post — often the cheapest units, and ones that never hit Zillow.
sources.gotcha: Now a statewide Vermont search, so filter by map. Classic scam territory: never wire money or pay a deposit before seeing the unit in person.

sources.name: Facebook Marketplace
sources.good_for: Fast-moving individual-landlord and sublet listings — good for catching units hours after they post.
sources.gotcha: Requires a Facebook login, and it has the highest scam density of any source here — fake landlords clone real listings, so verify ownership before sending anything.

sources.name: FB Group: Burlington, Vermont Area Rentals
sources.good_for: The big public rentals group — landlords and tenants post directly, including apartments that never get listed anywhere else.
sources.gotcha: A firehose of sublets, ISO posts, and the occasional scam. Same rule: no money before an in-person showing.

sources.name: FB Group: UVM Housing, Room, Rental & Sublet
sources.good_for: The student-market group — sublets, lease takeovers, and roommate openings near campus, busiest May through August.
sources.gotcha: Student-cycle timing: June 1 leases get signed six to nine months out, and many posts are rooms, not whole apartments.

sources.name: Front Porch Forum
sources.good_for: Neighbor-to-neighbor listings with real names attached — the highest-trust source in town, where quiet listings surface first.
sources.gotcha: You have to sign up for your neighborhood's forum and read the email digests — there's no browsing it from outside.

sources.name: UVM Off-Campus Housing
sources.good_for: UVM's official off-campus listing database — vetted landlord listings for the student and staff market, plus roommate matching.
sources.gotcha: Full search requires a UVM email; everyone else only sees the publicly indexed listing pages.

sources.name: Seven Days Classifieds
sources.good_for: The alt-weekly's classifieds — genuinely local, older-school landlords who don't use Zillow, plus sublet and housemate categories.
sources.gotcha: Thin inventory next to the national sites, statewide by default, and listings can sit stale.

sources.name: PadMapper
sources.good_for: Map-first aggregator — handy for visually scanning everything at once.
sources.gotcha: Almost pure aggregation, so listings lag and some are already rented; nothing here is exclusive.
